---
title: "Discussion Prompts"
---

Prompts for the facilitated discussion following the challenges overview and rapid presentations.

The aim is a cross-cutting conversation about shared data challenges rather than a deep dive into any single one.
For reference, see the guide's [challenges](../challenges/index.qmd) and [modelling options](../methods/index.qmd) pages.
We will take notes during the discussion and circulate a written summary after the session for your feedback.
If the room is large, we may split into smaller groups around flipcharts to give everyone space to contribute.

## Cross-cutting prompts

These are the main prompts we plan to use:

- Which of these challenges is the biggest barrier to timely analysis in your setting?
- What kinds of questions are you being asked that you cannot currently answer because of these data challenges?
- How do you currently handle recent incomplete data when briefing leadership?
- How do you currently monitor or assess the completeness of your data?
- Are there challenges, data or operational, you face that we have not covered?
- What would be most useful to you from this effort?

## Back-pocket prompts

We will keep these in reserve to prompt discussion if a topic stalls, rather than working through them in turn.

### Reporting delays

- What are the most common sources of [reporting delay](../resources/glossary.qmd#reporting-delay) in your jurisdiction?
- How long are typical delays for your main surveillance streams?
- Have you seen delays change over time (e.g. during outbreaks, holiday periods, system changes)?

### Data revisions

- How often do your data get revised after initial release?
- What causes revisions in your systems (deduplication, reclassification, date corrections)?
- Have you experienced large retrospective corrections that affected interpretation?

### Site drop-in and drop-out

- Do you have sites that report intermittently?
- How do you handle new sites joining or existing sites dropping out of reporting?
- Does this vary between surveillance streams?

### Other data quality issues

- What data quality issues most affect your ability to interpret recent trends?
- How do you handle missing dates or strata variables?
- Are there format or system compatibility issues that create apparent delays?
